title: Fermented Creamy Tomato Stir-fry
description: An energizing Portuguese dish featuring fermented cream and roasted tomatoes for a diabetic-friendly meal.
instructions: Cut the tomatoes into quarters and place them on a baking sheet.
Dice the onions and slice the garlic cloves thinly.
Place the onions, garlic, and tomatoes in a mixing bowl with a pinch of salt. Mix well.
Drizzle the olive oil over the tomato mixture and mix again until evenly coated.
Spread the seasoned mixture onto the lined baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes or until golden brown and crispy.
While the veggies are roasting, in a separate pan, heat up a bit of olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add roasted onions and garlic to the hot pan and cook for about 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in fermented cream into the onions and garlic mixture. Reduce heat to low and let simmer until thickened.
Serve warm with crusty bread or on its own as a side dish.
